verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease produced by prostate epithelium, released into seminal fluid and prostatic secretions with a fraction entering circulation. It has a signal peptide, no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor. Cell-state-induced: no surface display upon stress/activation documented. Tissue-restricted-surface: prostate-restricted but as a secreted product, not surface-anchored. Lysosomal exocytosis: not a lysosomal protein. Dual_localization: no membrane-anchored isoform. Stable_surface_attachment: no documented wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tether to a TM partner; though it cleaves PSMA and other surface substrates, this is transient enzymatic engagement, not stable anchoring. Therapeutic programs (e.g., JNJ-78278343 T-cell engager) target KLK2 captured by anti-KLK2 arm in prostate tumor microenvironment but KLK2 itself is secreted.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether local pericellular retention on prostate tumor cells (basis for KLK2-targeted bispecifics) constitutes wash-resistant stable_surface_attachment rather than transient secreted pool.
